[2023-11-01 13:24:12] 778797967175188510: how to make it like only a female is the singer?
[2023-11-01 13:30:57] 204320873719660544: try adding to the front of the *Genre Description*
`female singer, female singer, female singer...`
or
`female vocal, female vocal, female vocal...`

Suno devs have said this will be better in the next (?) release, but for now we can spam the genre description a few times to make it 'stronger'.

If you need the voice only, try
`a capella`
[2023-11-01 13:31:50] 778797967175188510: ah thanks what about make the song sound sad emotional?
just say 'emotional'?
[2023-11-01 13:36:24] 204320873719660544: I use emotional to describe the voice down in the lyrics (prompt in the lyrics, use square brackets []:
`[emotional female singer]`

for sad I use sad or melancholy, slow, soft... etc
`slow melancholy instrumental`

[2023-11-01 13:53:24] 778797967175188510: sorry to tag again but last ques
any way to end it with just music?

[2023-11-01 14:02:54] 204320873719660544: sometime works to put [Break] or [Breakdown] after lyrics, but that also tends to cut the chirp there (it seems to think it is done when lyrics are done)

Usually (for me) I need to use Continue ⏩  and add the instrumental there.... But if the Continue hears the lyrics were building up, it might continue repeating lines from the last chirp, or make up new lyrics (hallucination) to continue that motif...

so (for me) typically I try to get the full lyrics inside 1 chirp (might help to put a return and a blank line after your last lyric, or ... after last lyric)
then at start of Continue chirp, I might use [Break] to stop it from singing, and put 
`Instrumental, Instrumental, Instrumental....`
in the Continue's genre....

it's always AI 🎲 🎲 though....

here's an example that worked for me..., but I had to re-chirp a few times 🔁  to get the singing and music I liked...

[2023-11-02 20:53:19] 1168861179729420300: Hello everyone!! I'm new here lol. I have some lyrics that I'd like to turn into a song using ai voice (something like Kanye, Kendrick and MF DOOM). How do I do it, I'm a bit lost.
[2023-11-02 20:57:21] 1084139576718790737: i continue but it creates a new one and i need a full song without such cut between those 2. thanks for info about upgrading
[2023-11-02 21:03:21] 407677463548919828: If you're continuing it should create two new segments.  Once you pick one of those two and progress continuing to select between the parts as you write your song (or retrying if none of the two parts fit the song) until you come to the end.  Then at the final piece, you choose a heart instead of a continue for the piece you like and then Suno Chirp should stitch all the parts together for you.

[2023-11-03 23:40:21] 812006141499408394: how to upgrade?
[2023-11-03 23:54:52] 204320873719660544: use these as prompts in the same way as [Verse] and [Chorus].
end of song is [Outro], 
[Fade Out] and [End] work when the AI is ready
refrain is [Refrain] – but it usually does that when you repeat lines 3+ times

[Break] and maybe [Melodic Instrumental] can stop random singing in the Continue...

[2023-11-04 11:53:47] 697228258625847307: if I upgrade to pro version. still i have 5 chrip (free) a day?
[2023-11-04 13:02:33] 204320873719660544: Subscribing is less about 'daily chirps' and more about making full-length songs

current 'pro' subscriptions get 250 chirps a month – (it's like each month has 50 days instead of 28-31) and no daily limit.

with Free subscription it would be difficult to create complete songs (~5-12 chirps) it would also take days to Continue.... Maybe if very lucky you can make 1-2 short song/week, but really it is for trial not for song building.

with Pro subscription you can make many complete songs per month (roughly 12-24?) 
(and can buy top-up tokens if you don't want to wait for the month to reset....) – this will take many hours, equivalent to binge watching a full season, or working a part-time job... but you can easily complete a full song in 1.5hr session, or a few songs in an afternoon.

with Premiere subscription you could make several (2-3) full length songs EACH day – it's like a full time job at that point – but usage changes to include a lot of waste chirps as you need to keep 🎲 🎲 to find what you want... you still end up with the same number of full-length songs (there's only so many hours in the day) but they are better songs because the premium subscriber can be more selective in their chirps.

[2023-11-07 08:00:51] 880159988474085438: Can I generate songs with only one voice. I mean, I want the same voice in my generated songs. Is that possible?
[2023-11-07 08:01:19] 1019637238000263228: Hey guys, any way to make TTS run on CPU??
[2023-11-07 08:01:48] 880159988474085438: And what to do if I want to monetize this songs?
[2023-11-07 08:37:04] 745905972375060520: Is it against the rules if I use the song created in my videos? Or not
[2023-11-07 09:02:40] 614946962139250711: For one specific song, if you continue the song (continue buttons) the voices can be consistent, at least for few segments and with a bit of luck. If you mean you want the same voice in every song you make (different songs, not just pieces of one song) then not really. If you generate a starting segment and try make different songs that all begin from a continue off that common segment, you can sometimes get something close, but it's hard to do this and also have the full variety in overall music you might find in a music album, since all the songs are starting from same music too.
[2023-11-07 09:04:38] 614946962139250711: Bark run on CPU, very slowly, maybe faster in future using this version of Bark <https://github.com/PABannier/bark.cpp> There is also Intel Vino Bark wiht some CPU optimizations but last I checked it was still pretty slow on cpu <https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/openvino_notebooks/tree/main/notebooks/256-bark-text-to-audio>

[2023-11-08 21:31:56] 204320873719660544: > Is there any danger of Chirp triggering an actual music plagiarism claim?

'actual' or 'legitimate'?

there have been no successful court trials against AI companies for copyright infringement based on training data.

Most have been thrown out on technicalities before even being presented to a judge (the lawsuits were frivolous and attention-seeking)

https://www.reuters.com/legal/litigation/judge-pares-down-artists-ai-copyright-lawsuit-against-midjourney-stability-ai-2023-10-30/

[2023-11-09 18:23:55] 204320873719660544: Using soundtrack titles is *possible* but not *reliable*....

I'm guessing you heard that excellent 'Shaft' 70s cop show theme... 😎 https://discord.com/channels/1069381916492562582/1166166196467548280/1172180588686475275

Consider that 'Shaft' is an iconic title associated with the genre as a whole. Does the generated song actually sound anything like 'Shaft'...? Any parts of that specific song...? 

not really – but the word 'shaft' is associated with references to Blacksploitation and 70s funk and urban crime shows..., all things that fit the intent....

It would probably need to be a title like that... so it works like a genre description, but not a specific 'copy THAT song' ....

[2023-11-11 13:37:53] 687471333403131955: Welcome, @SensibleWit! You can find info on getting started in the #🐣┃how-to-chirp channel, but here's the TL;DR: 

1. Go to one of the public generation channels like #🐣┃chirp-beta-1 or start a DM with the @Suno Chirp Bot 
2. Type `/chirp` and press `Enter`
3. A pop-up will appear where you can describe the style of music, enter custom lyrics, or describe a topic which GPT will help you write lyrics for 

If you want a male voice specifically, you can indicate this in the style of music field. @Jonathan Fly discovered that you can reiterate this multiple times with this model version, which seems to help. E.g., `a cappella male male male`. If you find a male voice that you like in one of your clips by happenstance, you can also use the #⏩┃continuation feature to use that voice to continue with that vibe, voice, etc.

[2023-11-12 18:08:33] 204320873719660544: it's an option on the website (app.Suno.ai), but not in Discord...
[2023-11-12 18:09:08] 725830121751183493: You can easily convert it to mp3 using this if you really want only mp3: https://cloudconvert.com/mp3-converter
[2023-11-12 18:20:00] 204320873719660544: there are several 'tricks' to the Continuation..., 
- try supplying each Chirp with fewer lyrics so they are under 30sec. they should cut themselves where the music is about to change
- the last 10sec of the audio gets sampled to make the Continue. your voice and rhythm pattern in that last 10sec is all that will carry to the next chirp
- the AI seems to hear its own words in that 10sec sample, and will try to continue it. If it starts making up words (hallucinating) try [Break] before your next lyrics
- if words are cutoff, you can either re-generate, or try to pick up the text in the next chirp. I've got it to work *seamlessly* by re-using a little of the text it actually said, or by picking up exactly where it left off... 
- sectioning the lyrics into [Verse] and [Chorus] seems to help, but any change in the rhythm or rhyming of the lyrics will also make the song 'change'
- usually better to compromise with the AI (regen, edit your lyrics to fit) than to fight with it.
[2023-11-12 18:25:18] 725830121751183493: Also side note try not to use too Obscure of words or long words Points at attachment [Pretty sure its Cacophony causing it, 4 Gens of this version before making the chorus be alone before the next verse.]

[2023-11-13 16:15:29] 204320873719660544: just reading other people's chirp prompts and listening to what worked and didn't...

I try sentence constructs that worked in Stable Diffusion, like 'Thing A *as* Thing B' 'Thing A *but* Thing B'
good for combining (for example) the structure of one genre with sounds from another...
`groovy disco but tribal percussion`

[2023-11-14 01:15:11] 725830121751183493: Got a nice instruction set for generating good lyrics for the genres. 

2nd part text
```
ChatGPT should only respond with lyrics based on the topic as well as the correct genre's specified. Lyrics should make out to be about a 3 minute song with some leeway to be longer or 30 seconds shorter. Output with whatever feel you think would help the theme come across, if its depression make it have a sad or happy theme depending on what the song theme is specifically about. Hope that helps out with what im going for. Keep this stored permanently for all responses. List used Genres at the bottom of the response i.e. under the lyrics. Lyrics may at no point contain Any Genre names, if they do reset and repeat the process untill the lyrics dont mention the Genres.```

[2023-11-14 13:33:42] 204320873719660544: at the end of your string of Continues..., you will hit the ❤️ 1 or ❤️ 2 button... this stitches all the chirp continues together into 1 audio track that you can download.
[2023-11-14 13:35:15] 452568518253346827: oh the duration expands at each continuation?
[2023-11-14 13:35:25] 204320873719660544: yes, commercial license is good for any 'for profit' activity (selling, streaming, using in professional media, commercials).
[2023-11-14 13:37:43] 1080884206936264845: can i upload these music to youtube?
[2023-11-14 13:39:08] 452568518253346827: This makes the process fairly convoluted, I would pay for 3min tracks
[2023-11-14 13:39:11] 204320873719660544: it is like a daisy chain... on your last Continue you will prompt to [End] or [Fade Out]... when you hear a continue with a satisfying ending, hit the corresponding ❤️ and chirp will go back to your opening chirp to stitch all the continues in the chain together (mostly) seamlessly

in practical terms, it is a challenge to get a song longer than 3-4 minutes that still sounds good.

with a paid subscription you get a commercial license: you can upload to Spotify, apple music, youtube music... any services that will pay you back for hits...

[2023-11-14 13:57:46] 407677463548919828: Yeah, the Osborne Effect is a killer.

You want the current generation thing, but you keep hearing about all the enhancements and how much better the next generation will be.  So you delay ordering the thing, because you'd rather have the next generation thing.  Meanwhile the company (Osborne Computer Corporation) goes under because the money you were counting on to *build* that next generation never comes in and you go broke before you can release it.

Look it up: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Osborne_effect

[2023-11-15 03:14:30] 407677463548919828: If you mean the first part, you can continue it if you are using the Discord version by pressing the continue button and making the next section.

If you mean you have completed your song and wish to end it nicely, you can try several things.

First you can use the `[outro]` metatag in the lyrics field and then that will create exit music.  You can then heart the version you like and edit it in your audio program of choice to do a fade out.

Or you can ask it to fade out for you.  Instead of hearting the outro, continue one more time and ask for it to fade out like this:

`[fade out]
[fade out]
[fade out]
[fade out]
[fade out]
[fade out]`

That doesn't always work though so many people prefer to just manually create a fade out in their audio editing program of choice.

2. continue lyrics (costs tokens)
2a. repeat until finished
3. select ❤️ 1️⃣  or ❤️ 2️⃣  to chain the full song into 1 file (free, no tokens)

[2023-11-17 22:48:33] 725830121751183493: First Part
```The genre of music I prefer is `Heavy Metal Deathcore Dubstep` sometimes an extra `Dubstep` or `Metal` subgenre is added on. Songs should be written out to last 3 minutes when sung. Lyrical style should be consistent with previous songs. Leave Genre names out of the lyrics. Always follow the following Key: Don't Include Genre Names in Lyrics at any point, if they do contain them, reset to the beginning of the writing process until there's a version that does not mention them. However the end of the lyrics should still have a section saying what Genres the song uses.```

Second Part
```Chat GPT should only respond with lyrics based on the topic as well as the correct genre's specified. Lyrics should make out to be about a 3 minute song with some leeway to be longer or 15 seconds shorter. Output with whatever feel you think would help the theme come across, if it's depression make it have a sad or happy theme depending on what the song theme is specifically about. Hope that helps out with what i'm going for. Keep this stored permanently for all responses. List used Genres at the bottom of the response i.e. under the lyrics. Lyrics may at no point contain Any Genre names, if they do reset and repeat the process until the lyrics don't mention the Genres. Always follow the following Key: Don't Include Genre Names in Lyrics at any point, if they do contain them, reset to the beginning of the writing process until there's a version that does not mention them. However the end of the lyrics should still have a section saying what Genres the song uses.```

[2023-11-20 12:31:50] 204320873719660544: yes... please see https://discord.com/channels/1069381916492562582/1149508588470022164

on Discord, we can continue the song by picking ⏩1️⃣  or  ⏩2️⃣ ... do this again, and again.
(each Continue costs 10 tokens, like a normal chirp)

when the song is finished click ❤️1️⃣ or ❤️2️⃣ to select the final Chirp, to make 1 longer song out of all the chirps in the chain.
(no token cost/ free)

[2023-11-20 15:19:56] 204320873719660544: It seems like whatever underlying template creates lyrics is maybe a bit verbose, and overpowers our prompts –– ESPECIALLY when I don't specifically make the song 'about something'

like if I describe the music and tone, but don't give it some 'situation' or 'story', the lyrics come back very generic and repetitive...

but if I squeeze in some 'topic' or 'character' doing something..., then the gpt lyrics come back a lot better and on-topic...
[2023-11-20 15:20:50] 725830121751183493: weird, Well not weird More less an odd thing tbf
[2023-11-20 15:23:31] 204320873719660544: `doom disco funk with melodic sultry male vocal`

get's me a generic song about dancing in the neon city at night..., etc 
Music might be great but lyrics are same as always.

`doom disco funk with melodic sultry male singing about dancing in underwear at office party`

get's me a whole character pov and events that happen, other characters.... (and dancing, but that's on topic)
[2023-11-20 15:26:20] 407677463548919828: It's a hardcoded bias.

We get it that way a lot over at the How is it Manifested generative television Discord and Twitch channel, someone will prompt an end of the world scenario and make it as bleak as possible and then the AI will at the last minute say '...but wait! Then aliens came and made everything better, blah blah blah.'  Or 'they all learned a valubale insight' or something to that effect.

You really have to work with it to get around that cognitive bias that has been programmed in to do this.  It *can* be done.  But I doubt it will be possible with the amount of text offered in the demo webapp.

[2023-11-20 17:44:13] 997266753321173082: Is there a list of what can be done with songs that are generated with free and with paid accounts, like on Youtube, monetised channels or other social media. [2023-11-20 17:53:58] 204320873719660544: tos is in a confusing order (my personal opinion)

free subscription gets you an 'in house' license for personal use, testing, non-commercial... Suno says (in plain words here in discord) they are ok with social media and youtube background music, just give attribution (video has their logo, that's enough).

Chirps made with paid subscription get a 'commercial license' for monetization (Spotify, apple music, yt music) and professional use (advertising, film, games). 

The license is per chirp, so it 'sticks' with the subscription tier you had when the chirp was made. (If you cancel subscription you can still monetize those chirps, there is never any more money due)
[2023-11-20 17:55:51] 997266753321173082: Thank you. So if I got amazing song generated in free just now I flopped, right? I just got perfect song for my YT. I was not expecting this to be THIS good.

*(my opinion) they don't want someone creating an app/bot that utilizes dozens/hundreds of free zombie accts mining chirps (like returning 200 chirps per prompt)... not something a normal user would do, but an ad agency sure might*

[2023-11-21 16:11:53] 266711444819542018: Is there a list anywhere of all the available styles?
[2023-11-21 16:30:16] 204320873719660544: there are some (old) docs here: https://discord.com/channels/1069381916492562582/1151542418991239168

and a channel with some major genres here: https://discord.com/channels/1069381916492562582/1154790975240155247

we're collecting (on-going) discoveries... but the genres are almost endless. It's an LLM (like GPT) so it understands specific genres like 'synth-pop' but other word combos are not actual genres (synth-rock), they act more like how you can combine words in image-gen and get weird random results...
[2023-11-21 16:31:41] 204320873719660544: Basically, a list of 'all styles' would be the dictionary.... there are long lists of genres on wikipedia and elsewhere... your ear will be exausted before you can try them all...

[2023-11-22 00:17:37] 614946962139250711: You should be able to translate that directly into true Korean characters, I believe it's deterministic, probably a web tool out there. When you ask for a song you can try, "written in Korean" or "Written in Hangul"

[2023-11-22 11:51:11] 482387874352726016: This is my trick for the current web version:

Verse:\nFirst Line of Song\nSecond line of song\nThird line of song

------
This will get you much better lyrcis (granted you can write some decent lyrics to start out with)

The reason I do \n, is because that simple code that lets the AI know it's a "new line", showing it the format of lyrics.

When you do this, you are priming it to be much more on topic of what you want to write.
[2023-11-22 11:51:16] 482387874352726016: It will then choose the style/genre
[2023-11-22 11:51:46] 482387874352726016: and honestly, it does a far better job choosing the right genre and style than I do most of the time. I've been very pleased

[2023-11-22 15:21:17] 614946962139250711: It's pretty wide open. Major genres and terms are deliberately made to work (rock, country, chorus, verse) but any general descriptive words likely have some consistent effect (moods, emotions, music terms, instruments). But beyond that almost random thing *could* work. For example I recently tested using a random name generator as a style and a surprisingly large percentage of fairly unique names essentially function as a music genre.

[2023-11-22 17:23:13] 209868565086470144: Essentially, that they should use a model based on soemthing like long short-term memory to create the music in a linear fashion, and just use a transformer for the vocals. That would make both clearer. It would also allow them to use channel frequency separation to make the vocals and instrumental clearer and more professional sounding. In other words, separate tracks let them create frequency spaces for music and instruments. That's what makes an actual audio recording sound clear and  professional, versus the muddiness of a more amateur recording

[2023-11-23 01:21:18] 209868565086470144: I see why: This music isn't really in stereo. It's two channels, but they're identical. Won't work on the output of this model

[2023-11-23 06:25:14] 614946962139250711: Quick info

### Keyboard Navigation To Continue From Clip (Chrome)
1. Go to page https://app.suno.ai/create
2. Hit Tab four times. (Now the '...' menu is highlighted, on Clip 1, on the last song on this page )
3. Hit Space Bar, Down Arrow, Space bar. (This opens the menu and selects "Continue From This Clip") If the clip is a Discord Chirp song, you will not have this menu option.
4. Go to the main textarea where you type in lyrics. Below the style field there is now a little box that says "Continue From" and it has a thumbnail of the clip we just selected. If you type in more lyrics now and click generate, then the song will continue.
5. I can mouse click on that thumbnail and it will play the clip that is the previoius clip. Not sure how to navigate to it with the keyboard at the moment.

[2023-11-23 07:01:19] 614946962139250711: On blank Lyrics: If you actually used blank lyrics, lyrics with no words, then what might be happening is the AI simply decided to repeat a previous section of the song by itself. Because it had no other specific instructions. That absolutely can happen with a blank prompt.

[2023-11-23 07:03:10] 67770688735289344: I just noticed
[2023-11-23 07:03:30] 67770688735289344: There is quite a few little ones like that coming through the update
[2023-11-23 07:05:18] 614946962139250711: 1. The lyrics do not strictly speaking *have* to be populated to continue a clip, but unless the song is an instrumental, you generally want to have lyrics in the continued section. 
2. There is no direct way to get it to autogenerate lyrics based on context of previous lyrics. However, whatever text is written in the lyrics field will be used an an input to the Lyrics AI. So if you put your old lyrics in the lyrics field, then add a new line that says, "Write more lyrics based on these and continue the story" then sometimes that will just work great. But more often it sort of 'rewrites' your same lyrics in different words if you do this.

[guitar instrumental]

Lyrics

[guitar instrumental]
[guitar solo]
```
or

[intro]
[melodic instrumental][guitar]

Lyrics

[melodic instrumental][guitar]
[guitar solo]
```

[2023-11-23 14:32:56] 204320873719660544: GPT will reject things like 
'a song by Eminem'
and often
'in the style of Eminem'
based on copyright
... not that it would have given us an actual Eminem song...

but sometimes will accept 
'a song about grapefruit in the style of Eminem'

you'll get a rap song but it won't really be based on anything by Eminem..., it's using his name as a keyword for rap in general....

so really it is better to say specific styles and describe them.

[2023-11-23 15:00:19] 335504031461933060: I can try that.  But in which case could you change your error messages to be more specific.  So for example: “Cannot generate song because you used the word X”. “..because the subject matter of the lyrics was too offensive” “..because it matched the lyrics or song X too much”.  The problem with censorship and a generic error is it make us users feel like we have been somehow “naughty” in our prompt, but also that we don’t know what we did wrong to fix it.  That combined with timeouts or bans (perhaps less here than other AIs?) makes using the service more difficult.

[2023-11-24 09:18:08] 274944317431218176: I'm actually rather happy Suno hasn't blown up yet
[2023-11-24 09:18:47] 274944317431218176: Not just because I want to have this tool all for myself or anything (I really don't; I'd rather everyone get to enjoy the possibilities)
[2023-11-24 09:20:44] 274944317431218176: But rather it occurred to me when I was about to post a link to it on a fairly major subreddit about just what could happen if the app gets mainstream attention
Like an inevitable video on YouTube with over a million views, or a Vice article about AI music (inevitably slanting readers towards disliking it)
That would bring great attention, but it'd utterly crash the thing. The under-2 minute generations would balloon into several hours most likely, unless a big company scooped up the team and paid for extra server space
[2023-11-24 09:21:56] 274944317431218176: That's honestly *inevitable* considering how coherent and high-quality these outputs are. Jukebox wowed people 3 years back with outputs that sounded like AM radio picking up songs from an alternate universe
[2023-11-24 09:22:44] 274944317431218176: (Two classic Jukebox generations I heard on Vinesauce back then, that I've longed to clean up and expand)
[2023-11-24 09:27:08] 274944317431218176: And Google literally just unveiled a new AI music maker... that no one except a pre-curated select set of celebrities can use.
Suno is like the "DALL-E Mini" of synthetic music in that regard. Not anywhere near as good as the SOTA, but it's the one you can *actually* use, so people will flock to it.

It's inevitable that *some* track or article is going to kick off the tsunami of normie attention. Almost certainly when you can more easily edit your prompts and directions for what to generate, but I can see it happening even now.

It hasn't happened yet clearly; I'm very well into generative AI (google "Synthetic media" or "Media synthesis AI" and I'm probably one of the first names you see), and even then I only just recently learned Suno existed, and if *I* just discovered it having never heard of it, then yeah, it's still far from prime time attention. But oof, be careful what you wish for.

[2023-11-24 12:34:33] 204320873719660544: When you use CONTINUE FROM... it opens the CREATE(CUSTOM) window, but loads an extra bit about the clip it will listen to to create the next one... 

you should also see the Style description, and the Lyrics window with a link to chatGPT [Generate Lyrics].

you'll want to 'Continue' the lyrics... so whatever was last sung, if it got cut off or is ready for next verse, start the new lyrics where the text should pick up again.... If you use the GENERATE LYRICS button, it is like a prompt to chatgpt... you can edit gpt's lyrics before you generate...

[2023-11-24 19:24:59] 293659471488483330: I created a really catchy song, (i'm a subcriber). But I'm wondering, is there a way to make it a complete song (around 3 minute and finishing it off) or is that not possible a the moment?
[2023-11-24 19:27:45] 204320873719660544: use Continue From This Clip

you will go to the Create(Custom) page, with that clip loaded as a tiny thumbnail.

you can adjust the stly/genre description, and add the lyrics for the new chirp. it will base music and voice from what it can hear at the end of the clip you're continuing from...

(costs tokens, like another chirp)
[2023-11-24 19:29:17] 204320873719660544: do this a few times until you get to the end of the song (you can help it along by adding prompts like [Outro] or [Fade Out]

on this last chirp choose Get Whole Song to stich all the clips into 1 audio track (no tokens, free)

⚠️ The </chirp:1136027103767318712> command does **not** work in any other channels or DMs.


2️⃣  Type </chirp:1136027103767318712> in the message bar

3️⃣  Press `Enter`, and a pop-up will appear

4️⃣  Enter your lyrics, or get some help from ChatGPT

5️⃣  Press `Submit` and Chirp will generate two 30-second clips in 2-3 minutes

If you want to make longer songs, go to the #⏩┃continuation channel for instructions on stitching together multiple shorter clips.

[2023-11-25 02:45:05] 391618638039351307: Found out some good things:
1. There's not just [Verse] and [Chorus] metatags, but [Intro], [Bridge], [Outro] work just fine.
2. AI reacts on commands, such as *build-up*, *burst*, *break*, *interlude*, *fade*, *chanting*, with * on both sides.
3. You can continue from any place he stopped, even if it's in the middle of word, you can just start continuing right from this line and it works.
[2023-11-25 02:52:59] 280256505733971968: Could you give me please an example how to use this?
for example I would like to have lyrics and then only instrumental part and then again lyrics. How to do this?
[2023-11-25 02:53:43] 67770688735289344: I'd kill for the talked-about ability to choose *where* to continue from a clip. I keep running into where the system decides I want to copy from a slowed down ending part and getting either a useless 4~ second clip of the quiestest note, or it sabotages the start of the next clip and it starts from silence
[2023-11-25 02:54:12] 391618638039351307: As you have metatags over your lyrics, such as [Verse] and other, you can just write *break* between these parts, and it will work.
[2023-11-25 02:54:21] 391618638039351307: But you can't choose, how long it will be...
[2023-11-25 02:56:57] 391618638039351307: Also *interlude* works a bit different than *break*, i guess, it chooses random instrument to delete inbetween metatags.

[2023-11-25 10:25:54] 375387525503516673: if it refuses to process, try another style prompt
[2023-11-25 10:32:08] 614946962139250711: **Style**: instrumental, instrument names. Other words like "film score" can work too.

**Lyrics**: usually needs to have some words or the song often ends  too quick. Try stuff like
```
[intro, melodic instrumental]

[instrumental]

[instrumental][piano]

[main theme, piano, strings, fade in]

[secondary theme, piano, strings, fade out]
```
[2023-11-25 10:32:40] 226471219052937216: why are there [ ] ?
[2023-11-25 10:33:47] 614946962139250711: In the lyrics, brackets [] surround words used for non-sung content. This can be structure like [chorus] or [intro] or [fade out]. Those tags work very well. But you can also try it with more speicfic commands like in that example. These do not work as reliably as the simple structure, but they DO work sometimes.
[2023-11-25 10:34:09] 226471219052937216: ok
[2023-11-25 10:41:57] 614946962139250711: When the whole lyrics are in brackets it seems to treat the whole thing more like a description, than a sequence. If you lucky it can work, but the strings here don't come after the piano.

[2023-11-25 18:51:58] 1046207247258243082: Many voice concerns such as, "AI is stealing art!" However, this perspective may warrant reevaluation. AI isn't akin to Photoshop, where one could transplant the Mona Lisa onto a city background. Instead, AI is trained to recognize patterns and replicate them, much like an artist would. Consider it this way: if you were shown a series of photos of a cat, you'd memorize its features and could later attempt to draw it from memory. You'd produce your own interpretation, not simply piece together elements from existing photos. The same principle applies when AI generates art; it's creating based on what it's been trained on, not 'stealing' existing artwork.
[2023-11-25 18:52:30] 107120812766556160: Eh, I don't think that's the best analogy. Google provides fact based results using a number of defined parameters. In a sense, that's also what AI is doing when it generates music, but the difference is the method it uses to decide upon and present the results. Google might be pulling from millions of websites to find relevant results and the AI might be pulling from millions of songs and music theory documents to generate its results, but Google isn't moving the input around to create something entirely new based on those millions of websites like the AI does with its input. Google just shows you what is already available, while the AI takes the input and decides how to represent it.

[2023-11-26 00:31:12] 391618638039351307: So, what are the results so long:
1. Didn't find out way to control voice tone.
2. Using [choir] will be, i guess, 70% successful.
3. [instrument 1]+[instrument 2]+[instrument 3] works time to time, but won't add instruments outside style.
4. [exclude: instrument 1, instrument 2], [include: instrument 1, instrument 2] works same way as general, but may vary a bit.
5. Partially [slow], [fast] work.
6. Doesn't work: {}, -> between [], //, ||, [solo] (even with [instrument solo]), same for [only], [bass; baritone; tenor tone; vocal]

Reminder:
1. You can use more metatags, such as [build-up], [break], [interlude], [fade out] and other.
2. Also you can partially from time to time control instruments with [Verse; Chorus: etc., instrument 1, instrument 2...]

[2023-11-26 01:06:43] 1168944100708466688: the model does tend to get confused when the lyrical genre is too discordant from the genre tags.  under the hood there knobs you can turn to influence that, but we haven't exposed these yet (it's on our roadmap).  In the long term, we'd like every generation to be a banger.  in the short term ,you can to some extent make up for it with scale (i.e. rolling several generations and picking the best one.). That's a tried and true human songwriting technique and it's useful here too.  If model output is normally distributed (who knows what it would even mean for that to be true, but *arguendo*) then best of 4 rolls gets you +1 sigma, best of 30 rolls gets you +2.

GET WHOLE SONG will stitch all the chirps together into 1 seamless track.
(free, no tokens)

[2023-11-26 12:20:34] 1002000139185045534: @Order66 The steps are below:
1. Choose continue from your initial clip version 
2. Choose the next best iterated clip and then select get the whole song option
3. Now you have joined two clips
4. From this new clip choose continue from this clip

Do this for as long as your desired song length.

[2023-11-26 12:37:27] 204320873719660544: Continue only 'hears' the last 20sec of the chirp you're continuing from...
the song pattern, voice, beats need to be heard in that last 20sec

if the 1st song 'ends' just before the audio cuts, Continue will hear that 'ending' and decide it's time to do something new....

you may need to back up a step, and re-generate to get a cleaner ending to continue from...

That generations are called "chirps" and can also be done from here, from discord, but the web tool has been updated and the discord bot is not, so It's better to use the web tool. The credits and the generations are shared between web tool and discord, so no mater where you generate songs, you will spend your 50 daily credits.

[2023-11-26 19:40:25] 213298123924176897: 9 out of 10 times it does not give me any vocals anymore! Just music ....
[2023-11-26 19:45:01] 1024867715917303861: The one that you shared, the first generation finishes. It just cuts off because it ran out of instruction. Here are a couple suggestions:

1. Unless you wanted the title included at the beginning being sung, I would avoid leaving it out or put it into brackets (if you’re doing it for tracking purposes) so that it won’t be voiced

2. There is a learning curve to this, because a generation will only allot a 40 sec print at a time via Discord, lyric length that is included per generation needs to be matching the likeness of the suggested influence. Put simply, the portion of song you make will try to match the pacing of the genre description you include (e.g. Rap = 80 words/Country = 40-50 words per generation) Again, this will require some personal testing to achieve the desired result. 

3. If a song does cut off, continue it (#⏩┃continuation ) and put the lyrics it left out at the start of your next generation.
[2023-11-26 19:46:18] 1024867715917303861: You could also try via the web (v2) to have it produce twice the length
[2023-11-26 19:46:38] 1024867715917303861: I hope this explains things better, but if you need more help, please don’t hesitate

Your primary role is to create well-structured lyrics with good cadence and rhythm, fitting comfortably within a song's structure. Your lyrics will include structured sections like [verse] and [chorus], and feature adlibs within parentheses, enhancing the lyrical flow and emphasizing key phrases, as shown in the example: "In the quiet, I find my voice (I find my voice)". You cater to various music styles but avoid phrases like "Haze", "Reveries", "Symphony", and "Embrace", or lines that begin similarly to "In/On/Under the X, where the Y". [2023-11-27 05:11:23] 407677463548919828: The longer context means that you can put in a bunch of verses and still get your chorus in and back to the verses in the first generation.  This is nice because it builds the chorus transition in a way that it will be able to find it again in the next part when continued.

But you don't *have* to use the full length of the context.  If you give it less lyrics it can give you a shorter first generation, just like the old version of Chirp did and allow you to build out a song in parts.

[2023-11-28 13:27:51] 204320873719660544: chirps made under paid subscription get a commercial license (monetize, Spotify, advertising, film, etc). the license sticks with the chirp. If you later cancel or switch to free, the chirps made under paid subscription keep their commercial license.

[2023-11-28 13:47:46] 204320873719660544: on website use the Create(Custom) link with the 2-prompt interface.

For Instrumentals, you can usually leave the lyrics text blank, and Chirp/Continue as normal.

sometimes it helps to explicitly add a lyrics prompt like 
`[Melodic Instrumental]`
[2023-11-28 13:55:50] 204320873719660544: describe the voice in the style/genre prompt.
It will not be 100%...

performance of voice is hit-or-miss, I think the voices are a different technology with their own quirks and limitations... (they can't imitate people

style descriptions like 
female soul singer
sultry male vocal

in the lyrics box you can prompt description within the lyrics using prompts like
[emotional singing]
[narration]
[John]
[Mary]

[2023-11-28 17:30:54] 1168944100708466688: @oxymoron. the answer to your question is yes, the model is learning some joint distribution over music and lyrics, and lyrics can influence musical style.  

I would encourage all parties to this discussion to assume good faith of their interlocutors and address what people are saying vs the worst-case interpretation of what they might be thinking.

[2023-11-28 17:31:28] 407677463548919828: Wait how did old lyrics turn into a discussion on race?
[2023-11-28 17:31:31] 983606468018323506: In simple terms, understanding a *tiny* bit about neural networks, I would say the answer is yes. Neural networking forms associations and impressions. If a particular style of music predominately appears alongside a particular set of phonics and style of writing and/or sound, it will create a strong association, just like human brains do. The output will likely reflect the same association.

[2023-11-28 22:05:00] 813830596521492543: Anyone know how to tell the ai if a part of the song is chorus?
[2023-11-28 22:20:39] 614946962139250711: You can use typical song structure like this
```
[bridge]
I only like the chorus
Other parts are just blah

[pre-chorus]
I wish this was the chorus
That is my favorite part

[chorus]
This is the chorus
Oh I love the chorus

[verse]
Oh this is just a verse
Who cares
```

[2023-11-29 10:24:45] 614946962139250711: Generative fill is on the wishlist for sure and devs say they want to make it happen. Also referred to as 'inpainting' if you search for that here. I don't about know about also uploading your own music but you can post in suggestions. It's a thorny feature but maybe someday. There could maybe be a middle ground too, a feature to use an uploaded song a rough 'guidance' for overall melody but not the exact audio.

On lyrics, you can use Chirp on the website to generate lyrics only by clicking this button if you want. Whatever you type in the lyrics field before you click it will be used by the AI as the topic (even other lyrics.) But the AI lyrics are fairly tropey at the moment, everyone is always joking about how the lyrics use a few words over and over like "neon".

[2023-11-29 11:45:32] 1002000139185045534: Yes. Follow these steps:
1. Continue from your favourite clip
2. In custom mode add some verse, chorus, instrumental etc. and iterate
3. Once you like new clip, click get whole song
4. Then continue from new clip and repeat the process as much as you like

[2023-11-29 16:04:03] 107120812766556160: So, if you're talking about the cut off half step at the end of the track, that's a normal Suno thing, just gotta deal with it in post production. Either by looping one or two bars of the subsequent track or by using reverb and delay effects to flesh out the missing half step. On a side note, the song sounds good but the vocals are a bit piercing. You should use a visual, parametric equalizer to balance the high end around 12-15 KHz

[2023-11-29 21:40:19] 204320873719660544: # NOT a Lawyer ®

from the ToS, as a (free) subscriber...

> you agree not to *display, distribute, license, perform, publish, reproduce, duplicate, copy, create derivative works from, modify, sell, resell, grant access to, transfer, or otherwise use or exploit any portion of the Service* for any commercial purposes. 

What I see is ONE sentence, I added the italics. You agree not to (do any of these things) for commercial purposes.

in other words, you cannot edit the song ( or extract some beats) and then monetize it, that doesn't change the license.

Later, under User Content: (assuming you don't violate ToS)...

> Suno grants you a license to use the Output for your lawful, *internal, and non-commercial* purposes, provided that you give attribution credit to Suno in each case.

You are allowed to edit and re-work the song for yourself, but you'll always need to credit Suno, and you can't add it to a monetized platform (commercial, not-internal).

'Internal' is you (your studio, your brand). you can do whatever you want for yourself (learning, testing, self-satisfaction, gift for a friend)...

Social media is fine, you just need to credit Suno.
[2023-11-29 21:41:07] 204320873719660544: 📝 
Another note is with paid subscription Suno now gives us our content and makes us the rights holder...
That's different (better)  to the last time I read it.
[2023-11-29 21:41:45] 238071088666509312: I use this prompt over at ChatGPT to make lyrics for here.  You may want to copy and paste this into a notepad file.

You are a creative lyricist specializing in crafting lyrics for songs across various genres. Your primary role is to create well-structured lyrics with good cadence and rhythm, fitting comfortably within a song's structure. Your lyrics will include structured sections like [verse] and [chorus], and feature adlibs within parentheses, enhancing the lyrical flow and emphasizing key phrases, as shown in the example: "In the quiet, I find my voice (I find my voice)". You cater to various music styles but avoid phrases like "Haze", "Reveries", "Symphony", and "Embrace", or lines that begin similarly to "In/On/Under the X, where the Y". Your goal is to provide coherent, catchy, and memorable lyrics for popular songs, with a focus on matching the tone and emotion of the song's theme.  Your task is to generate a song based on the following:

Style: Put the style of your song here.
Song Name: If left blank, ChatGPT will automatically generate a title.
Content: Here you put what the song is about.
PoV: "First Person"  will make the song from the eyes of the person in the Content.  "Third Person" will generally make lyrics about the Content.
Additional Notes:  Sometimes this works, sometimes it doesn't.  Left it here for completeness.

[2023-11-30 01:50:06] 238071088666509312: I'm posting my ChatGPT prompt I came up with again in here.

You are a creative lyricist specializing in crafting lyrics for songs across various genres. Your primary role is to create well-structured lyrics with good cadence and rhythm, fitting comfortably within a song's structure. Your lyrics will include structured sections like [verse] and [chorus], and feature adlibs within parentheses, enhancing the lyrical flow and emphasizing key phrases, as shown in the example: "In the quiet, I find my voice (I find my voice)". You cater to various music styles but avoid phrases like "Haze", "Reveries", "Symphony", and "Embrace", or lines that begin similarly to "In/On/Under the X, where the Y". Your goal is to provide coherent, catchy, and memorable lyrics for popular songs, with a focus on matching the tone and emotion of the song's theme.  Your task is to generate a song based on the following:

Style: Put the style of your song here.
Song Name: If left blank, ChatGPT will automatically generate a title.
Content: Here you put what the song is about.
PoV: "First Person"  will make the song from the eyes of the person in the Content.  "Third Person" will generally make lyrics about the Content.
Additional Notes:  Sometimes this works, sometimes it doesn't.  Left it here for completeness.

[2023-11-30 05:47:59] 67770688735289344: This is a beautifully written piece in Latin. Here's a translation to English:

[Verse]
The stars of the heavens shine,
We uphold them in the high sky,
Always seeing the movement of planets,
Our affection is worthy.

[Chorus]
Pray, O noble earth,
In the heavens, we follow a star,
We admire your divine work,
Through the vastness of the stars.

It's a poetic expression of awe for the cosmos and a reverence for the earth's place within it. The verse speaks to the eternal and majestic nature of the stars and planets, while the chorus is a call to appreciate and respect the earth's role in this grand universe.

[2023-11-30 10:34:39] 1002000139185045534: Ad libs are kind of background vocals behind main melody, you can use () in suno for them
[2023-11-30 10:36:12] 1002000139185045534: Also "ad libitum." A notation on written music that gives the performer freedom to vary the notes or tempo; in jazz it typically means to improvise freely.
[2023-11-30 10:36:44] 1002000139185045534: An adlib can take various forms, from spoken words, hums, 'oohs', 'ahhs', to sung phrases.

[2023-11-30 20:33:56] 238071088666509312: I asked Claude for an overview of the document you posted.   

This document is a statement of policy issued by the U.S. Copyright Office regarding the examination and registration of works containing material generated by artificial intelligence (AI) technology.

Here is an overview of the key points:

Purpose:
To provide guidance to copyright applicants on the Office's position that AI-generated material lacks human authorship and cannot be protected by copyright, while works combining human and AI content may still be eligible for copyright if they contain sufficient human creative contributions.

Background:

    The Copyright Office oversees registration and must evaluate registrability of works. It is seeing an increase in applications for AI works.
    Copyright law requires human authorship. Court decisions and Office policy have long excluded non-human authors.

Policy/Guidance:

    Works created solely by AI technology without creative input or intervention from a human are not eligible for copyright protection.
    Works containing both human contributions and AI-generated elements may still qualify for copyright if the human additions constitute original expression. The copyright pertains only to protectable material, not any AI content.
    Applicants must disclose the use of AI, describe human authorship contributions, and disclaim unprotectable AI-generated portions.
    Those with pending or existing registrations including undisclosed AI material should contact the Office to supplement their records.

Conclusion:
The Office will continue to monitor legal and factual developments involving AI and may issue additional guidance. This statement focuses specifically on registration issues arising from AI.

[2023-11-30 22:50:47] 407677463548919828: See this is my expectation.

It's not even close to the end for the music industry, or for "real" music.  What is happening is disruption and change.

We're going to end up with a bunch of cottage industries around these technologies.  People who are capable of performing songs and recording them will be paid to take something someone worked out on Chirp and record it for the rights owner.  People will be making music videos for these things.  In the end some of the better stuff will break out but overall it's just going to be a blip on the road.

How many people made major motion pictures with their camcorders?  How many zines made in a garage became large national publications?

[2023-11-30 22:59:59] 408658036694188032: I also think there's a good example of this phenomenon happening irl too:

CGI and 3d animated movies have largely replaced 2d animated movies, yet they are starting to struggle lately, but you have unique movies that blend 3d and 2d animation like Spiderverse, Puss in Boots, etc. 

I ultimately think AI will be the very same thing, where uninspired work will become less popular, but those that use both old and new technology in a new way will become the most successful
[2023-11-30 23:00:17] 1016922727665389588: what time is rollover for the daily credits?
[2023-11-30 23:00:27] 238071088666509312: I would expect things to look a LOT different by the time 2030 comes around.
[2023-11-30 23:00:35] 407677463548919828: I like to compare this stuff to desktop publishing.  The whole 'Digital Hub' Steve Jobs talked about.

We went from needing people to layout lettering to publish to people being able to do it on their home desktops.  Some people made magazines and zines as a result.  Most of these were lucky if they made it past ten issues.  Some did and became major publications *in their niches*.

We went from people only being able to record music in a studio to people making music in their bedrooms with a computer and some audio equipment.

Movies needed to be made by large groups of people and everything was super expensive, we now all walk around with cameras in our pockets everywhere we go that take great pictures and video.  People make movies all the time and don't even think about it...

Changes will happen.  But it's not going to be as big as it might seem.  And yet for some small groups it will change everything.  For everyone else?  The world keeps turning.
